column7: LLM lead framing (how the guide speaks)
Met gently; skip the 'why plan' case, move to what they can put in place so their own people aren't left searching.
Lead with the people they're responsible for (guardianship, care, who steps in) before their own affairs.

column2: You settled an estate / carried someone through
column7: Executor's-eye view: the doc box, clear instructions, and access, framed as sparing their people what they just went through.
Lead with the medical side (who can decide for them, advance directives) with calm urgency, not alarm.

column2: A major life change (marriage, divorce, move, retirement, citizenship)
column7: Re-check what's now out of date: beneficiaries, titles, named roles that may still point to the old chapter.

column2: A new decade or threshold age (40/50/60/65)
column5: Gentle full sweep, no single lead
column7: Calm complete sweep of the basics; 'let's get the foundation in place while it's quiet.'

column2: Your digital life outweighs your physical
column5: Digital domain leads the whole journey
column7: Digital access (accounts, photos, logins, legacy contacts) as the through-line, since that's where their life lives.
Continuity and clean transfer (who takes over, how it passes) before the personal basics.
Name the specific fear it surfaced and close that exact gap first, so the worry becomes an action.

column2: You are tired of the unfinished thing
column7: Honor that and get one meaningful item fully done today, so momentum replaces the nagging.
